label.title: Spectral Mean Normalization for Speech Recognition
label.summary: Describes an experiment using long-term spectral mean normalization over a six-second window to improve speech recognition in reverberant and far-mic conditions. Tested with HTK on clean TI-digits training, the method dramatically reduced error rates in both simulated reverberation and real meeting recorder scenarios.

Normalizes the current center frame by mean subtraction; all means calculated first, then subtraction applied

label.title: Remote Control Interface Design Concept
label.summary: A design discussion for a TV remote control featuring a large touchscreen with clear primary buttons and a changeable menu structure. The core principle is keeping the interface simple, ergonomic, and free of unnecessary functions and menu levels.
key_points: Concept uses one big touchscreen with primary buttons always visible and clearly displayed
Menu structure should be changeable - pressing 'system' reveals multiple system options
Remote should be held in one hand, made a little broader rather than thin (thumb operation deemed too difficult for full control)
Thumb used on main buttons, other hand on menu structure
Layout includes program number buttons (1,2,3,4...), volume control, and program control
Bottom area could feature one big LCD for the menu structure
Avoid too many functions displayed at the same time
When programming a specific setting (e.g., TV color), don't show 20 unrelated options
Avoid too many menu levels - aim for one-button access where possible
For channels above 10, use a '1-0' entry method like modern TVs to save a button
Secondary functions like teletext should go in the menu

Design remote to be broader for comfortable one-handed grip
Include program buttons, volume control, and program control in the layout
Implement a large LCD for the menu structure at the bottom
Simplify menus to minimize levels and number of button presses
questions: How should the remote's overall form/look be finalized?
Which functions belong on physical buttons versus in the menu?
